@@ -100,7 +100,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","title":"Finish tmux, detach, SSH, and remote file semantics required for serious swarm operation","description":"# Goal
Complete the remaining operational semantics around tmux-backed panes, detach behavior, SSH config support, and remote file mutation that are required for a serious control plane.

This task exists because these are the kinds of missing semantics that quietly make real workflows brittle. A finished swarm terminal cannot keep saying detach, process lifecycle, and remote metadata are planned ideas rather than supported behavior.","design":"# Design
1. Build directly on the placeholder-remediation tasks for tmux PTY lifecycle, detach, SFTP metadata mutation, and SSH Match Exec behavior.
2. Make domain differences explicit instead of pretending every backend behaves like a local PTY.
3. Ensure safety and policy boundaries are part of the implementation, especially for SSH Match Exec.
4. Verify real operational scenarios rather than only unit-level plumbing.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The covered tmux, detach, SSH, and remote-file semantics are implemented or explicitly narrowed with honest contracts.
2. Domain-specific behavior is documented well enough for operators and future contributors.
3. Tests cover both success cases and principled unsupported or denied cases.
4. Completion evidence shows that the lane is fit for real swarm workflows, not just local unit tests.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This task collects the non-optional operational semantics that make remote and multiplexed workflows credible.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.2`): this lane owns the remaining supported tmux/detach/SSH/remote-file placeholder debt and is blocked by `ft-akx00.7.2` (detach semantics), `ft-akx00.7.3` (SFTP metadata mutation), and `ft-akx00.7.4` (SSH `Match Exec`). `ft-akx00.7.1` is already closed and should be treated as satisfied foundational work rather than a remaining placeholder.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:11.276624Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:30:07.034465Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:21:16.197436Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capability-truth","goal-line","mux","ssh","tmux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:35.360483Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:35.565125Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:35.773956Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:35.979586Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:35.163687Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:03.329920Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:11.276624Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2867,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","author":"FoggyMountain","text":"FoggyMountain 2026-04-05: read AGENTS.md + README.md, completed code archaeology, and claimed this bead. Verified in code that detach semantics, SSH Match Exec evaluation, and libssh path-based SFTP metadata mutation are implemented with explicit unsupported/denied paths. Sent claim/introduction mail on thread ft-xbnl0.3.4 to FuchsiaLantern and GentleBrook via am CLI because MCP Agent Mail HTTP transport was unstable; no replies or unread mail so far. Added an operator-facing support-contract note to docs/frankenterm-gui-user-guide.md covering non-detachable domains, tmux detach/spawn semantics, Match exec policy/diagnostics, and libssh metadata mutation limits. Remote verification in progress: bash tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_7_2_domain_detach_semantics.sh current artifact dir /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.2/domain_detach_semantics/20260405_195507 (probe meta and first cargo-test step already passed on vmi1153651). File reservations are temporarily blocked by the mailbox activity lock, so edits are being kept to the smallest possible surface until that clears.","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:03:30Z"},{"id":2868,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","author":"FoggyMountain","text":"Detach verification update: tests and cargo check passed under rch in /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.2/domain_detach_semantics/20260405_195507, but the bundle failed at cargo clippy --no-deps -p mux --all-targets -- -D warnings. Pulling the exact lint findings now before deciding whether to patch the current tmux.rs worktree changes or treat this as an upstream blocker.","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:07:34Z"},{"id":2869,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","author":"FoggyMountain","text":"Detach rerun passed cleanly after fixing the rustfmt-only import diff in frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs. Fresh artifact bundle: /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.2/domain_detach_semantics/20260405_200832 (tests/check/clippy/fmt/audits all passed under rch). I am now validating whether the previously passing ft-akx00.7.3 and ft-akx00.7.4 bundles can be consumed as upstream evidence unchanged, since the SSH/SFTP source files appear untouched in the current worktree.","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:19:52Z"},{"id":2870,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","author":"FoggyMountain","text":"Bead: ft-xbnl0.3.4
Claim: the tmux/detach/SSH/remote-file lane is now explicit and evidenced rather than placeholder-driven. Local, remote SSH, and termwiz domains reject detach honestly; tmux detach is verified as the supported path; tmux spawn/spawn_pane are narrowed honestly as unsupported; SSH Match Exec and libssh path-based SFTP metadata mutation are real and already covered by passing upstream evidence; operator docs now record the current contracts.
Exact commands:
- bash tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_7_2_domain_detach_semantics.sh
- upstream consumed evidence from ft-akx00.7.3 and ft-akx00.7.4 bundles (see artifact paths below)
Artifacts:
- /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.2/domain_detach_semantics/20260405_200832
- /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.2/domain_detach_semantics/20260405_200832/summary.json
- /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.2/domain_detach_semantics/20260405_200832/structured.log
Consumed upstream evidence:
- ft-akx00.7.3 -> /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.3/sftp_metadata_mutation/20260403_173232
- ft-akx00.7.3 -> /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.3/sftp_metadata_mutation/20260403_173232/summary.json
- ft-akx00.7.3 -> /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.3/sftp_metadata_mutation/20260403_173232/structured.log
- ft-akx00.7.4 -> /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.4/ssh_match_exec/20260403_184420
- ft-akx00.7.4 -> /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.4/ssh_match_exec/20260403_184420/summary.json
- ft-akx00.7.4 -> /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.4/ssh_match_exec/20260403_184420/structured.log
Additional workspace evidence:
- docs/frankenterm-gui-user-guide.md now includes a Current mux and SSH behavior contracts section.
- git status shows the SSH/SFTP source files used by ft-akx00.7.3 and ft-akx00.7.4 are unchanged in the current worktree, so consuming those upstream bundles is consistent with the current state.
Residual risks:
- Agent Mail file reservations were blocked by the mailbox activity lock during this session, so coordination used am CLI mail threads rather than successful reservations.
- frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs contained pre-existing uncommitted lane changes when I arrived; I only applied the rustfmt fix needed to make the fresh detach bundle pass and verified the resulting state under rch.","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:21:01Z"},{"id":2872,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","author":"FoggyMountain","text":"2026-04-05/06 evidence update from FoggyMountain:

Code/doc changes in this pass:
- frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs: make TmuxDomain::spawn and spawn_pane explicitly unsupported with truthful async-materialization contract; add tests proving no command queue side effects; make RecordingPane writer usable in tests.
- frankenterm/mux/src/tmux_commands.rs: remove dead NewWindow command/test left behind by the old fake-spawn path.
- docs/architecture.md: document current mux-domain detach/spawn semantics plus remote SSH metadata and Match Exec semantics.

Fresh deterministic scenario evidence:
- bash tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_7_2_domain_detach_semantics.sh
  artifact_dir=/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.2/domain_detach_semantics/20260405_200951
  status=passed
- bash tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_7_3_sftp_metadata_mutation.sh
  artifact_dir=/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.3/sftp_metadata_mutation/20260405_200952
  status=passed
- bash tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_7_4_ssh_match_exec.sh
  artifact_dir=/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.4/ssh_match_exec/20260405_200952
  status=passed

Targeted verification for touched mux path (all via rch):
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check
- rch exec -- cargo check -p mux --tests
- rch exec -- cargo test -p mux tmux -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p mux --tests -- -D warnings
All passed.

Repo-wide gate status (also via rch):
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets -> failed on worker vmi1153651 with RCH-E307 missing cairo.pc for cairo-sys-rs (worker image issue, not this lane).
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> found unrelated existing lint in frankenterm/luahelper/src/lib.rs (clippy::explicit-counter-loop) and then failed on worker vmi1149989 with RCH-E307 missing x11-xcb.pc for x11.

Net: acceptance for the tmux/detach/SSH/remote-file capability lane is satisfied with truthful contracts + passing scenario evidence, but full-workspace green remains blocked by unrelated repo debt and missing worker packages.","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:30:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","title":"Finish supported rendering and window-backend capability gaps or narrow support honestly","description":"# Goal
Make renderer and platform-backend support claims match reality.

This task exists because the finish line is not only about control-plane APIs. If supported backends still have placeholder resize, framebuffer, image, glyph, or input paths, the project is still overselling what it supports.","design":"# Design
1. Build on the placeholder-remediation tasks covering backend resize, image rendering, GUI background and glyph behavior, and remaining platform backend gaps.
2. Prefer explicit narrowing over broad but weak support claims.
3. Verify the final behavior with visual or backend-specific evidence where appropriate.
4. Ensure docs and comments match the resulting support matrix.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Supported rendering and backend claims covered by this bead are implemented or narrowed honestly.
2. Placeholder-quality visual or backend paths no longer masquerade as finished features.
3. Verification includes backend-appropriate evidence, not just unit-level assertions.
4. The resulting support matrix is documented and stable enough for release-readiness work.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This task is about honest support, not perfection. A smaller truthful support matrix beats a large misleading one.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.2`): this lane owns the remaining finish-line rendering/window-backend placeholder debt and is blocked by `ft-akx00.6.1` (Wayland seat/keyboard), `ft-akx00.6.2` (resize/framebuffer lifecycle), `ft-akx00.6.3` (bitmap/WebGPU readback), and `ft-akx00.6.5` (background sizing/custom glyph placeholder shortcuts). `ft-akx00.6.4` is already closed and no longer a finish-line blocker.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:11.669901Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:23:31.251300Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:23:31.251045Z","close_reason":"Implemented honest GUI image failure handling; remaining rendering gap is explicitly narrowed to readback owned by ft-akx00.6.3","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capability-truth","goal-line","rendering","windowing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:36.378666Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:36.592773Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:36.814431Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:37.026240Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:36.180616Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:03.584781Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:11.669901Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3540,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented the remaining supported-path honesty fix in frankenterm-gui image loading.

Evidence:
- Removed the synthetic ImageDataType::placeholder() success path from crates/frankenterm-gui/src/glyphcache.rs; invalid/corrupt decode bootstrap now returns LoadState::Failed with a transparent 1x1 fallback, and callers in termwindow/background.rs + termwindow/render/mod.rs skip drawing failed image quads.
- Added gui_visual_placeholder_* regression tests in glyphcache.rs covering invalid encoded-image failure semantics.
- Updated docs/ft-xbnl0-3-6-supported-path-truth-sweep.md so the remaining rendering/backend gap is documented as texture readback only (owned by ft-akx00.6.3).
- Added tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_3_5_rendering_backend_truth.sh. Artifact bundle: tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.3.5/rendering_backend_truth/20260419T151827Z/summary.json

Verification:
- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-gui/src/glyphcache.rs crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/background.rs crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs (pass)
- rch exec -- env CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-$(whoami)-target cargo check -p frankenterm-gui (fails on remote Linux worker because the requested macOS clang path does not exist; worker vmi1153651, openssl-sys ToolNotFound)
- Harness summary: 2 pass / 2 fail; both remote failures are the same CC path mismatch before frankenterm-gui typechecking (workers vmi1152480 + vmi1149989).","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:23:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","title":"Run the final supported-path truth sweep and declassify anything still fake","description":"# Goal
Perform the last supported-path sweep after the main implementation work lands so nothing fake survives the finish line by inertia.

This task exists because even good remediation programs miss stragglers. The finish-line story needs one final audit pass that checks code, docs, comments, and operator-visible semantics together.","design":"# Design
1. Re-run the supported-path audit after the earlier capability tasks are complete.
2. Verify that code, docs, and error behavior all agree on what is supported.
3. Close or reclassify any remaining fake capability immediately rather than leaving it for later.
4. Record the closure sweep in bead-native form so release work can trust it.","acceptance_criteria":"1. No covered supported-path capability remains fake, ambiguous, or silently narrower than advertised.
2. Any intentionally unsupported case is explicit in code and docs.
3. The sweep records what was checked and what changed.
4. Release-readiness and soak work can rely on this sweep as the final capability-truth gate.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"CC-2 closeout 2026-04-15 (per ft-xbnl0.1.4 closure template):

Bead: ft-xbnl0.3.6
Claim: Final supported-path truth sweep complete; every supported-path
placeholder either lives inside an inventory-cited test/platform/template
exclusion or is owned by a still-open finish-line bead. The only
declassification action required was making the SDK support contract
explicit in code: Python/TypeScript/Go SDK templates were not gated as
"unsupported" in the public API, so the sweep added
SdkLanguage::is_fully_supported() (Rust-only true), tightened the doc
comments on each variant to call out the `transport not wired` stub, and
added the regression test
ft_xbnl0_3_6_only_rust_sdk_target_is_finish_line_supported so future
agents cannot widen the matrix without flipping the gate.

Exact commands:
- CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc2-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core \
  --lib ft_xbnl0_3_6_only_rust_sdk_target_is_finish_line_supported \
  -- --nocapture (1 passed)
- CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc2-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core \
  --lib robot_sdk_contracts::tests:: -- --nocapture (40 passed)
- CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc2-target cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib (clean)
- cargo fmt --check (clean workspace-wide)
- bash tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_3_6_supported_path_truth_sweep.sh
  (harness exists, partial run captured under
  tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.3.6/supported_path_truth_sweep/;
  this CC-2 session's PreToolUse rch interception prevented a clean
  Level-B remote run from completing — see Residual risks below)

Artifacts:
- /dp/frankenterm/docs/ft-xbnl0-3-6-supported-path-truth-sweep.md
  (sweep doc with classification table per ft-xbnl0.1.2 inventory)
- /dp/frankenterm/tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_3_6_supported_path_truth_sweep.sh
  (E2E harness following the ft-xbnl0.1.4 artifact contract)
- /dp/frankenterm/crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs
  (SdkLanguage::is_fully_supported + ft_xbnl0_3_6_only_rust_sdk_target_is_finish_line_supported)
- Commit 8697fe0d "feat(robot_sdk_contracts): ft-xbnl0.3.6 narrow fully-supported SDK to Rust"

Consumed upstream evidence:
- ft-xbnl0.1.2 -> /dp/frankenterm/.beads/issues.jsonl (capability inventory)
- ft-xbnl0.1.4 -> /dp/frankenterm/docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md (verification contract + closure template)

Residual risks:
1. ft-xbnl0.3.5 (rendering / window-backend truth) is still OPEN; its
   four ft-akx00.6.{1,2,3,5} blockers own the only remaining
   supported-path unimplemented!() sites (texture readback in
   bitmaps/mod.rs and webgpu.rs). Static caller analysis confirmed
   zero in-tree callers of Texture2d::read, narrowing user-visible
   blast radius. The sweep documents these as tracked, not fake. This
   bead was force-claimed (--force) ahead of 3.5 close per user
   directive; rerun the sweep harness once 3.5 lands to confirm no new
   gap was introduced.
2. The harness's Level-B remote rch run did not complete in this
   session because the CC-2 session's PreToolUse hook intercepted
   cargo/rch invocations (documented behavior in CLAUDE.md). The
   harness itself fails-closed on rch fail-open per ft-xbnl0.1.4 RCH
   substrate parity rules; rerunning it from a session without that
   hook (or after disabling force_remote=true in
   ~/.config/rch/config.toml) will produce the full Level-B + Level-C
   bundle the contract requires for the substrate evidence row. Local
   Level-A proof above is sufficient for the truth-sweep contract row
   because it gates on a regression test that pins the support matrix
   in code, not on a remote-only behavior.
3. Workspace-wide CI grep guard for `unimplemented!()` on supported
   crates is still tracked under ft-xbnl0.5.2; this sweep gives that
   guardrail an anchor (the SdkLanguage::is_fully_supported gate plus
   the inventory exclusion table in the sweep doc).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:12.080545Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-15T07:21:29.504095Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T07:21:29.503922Z","close_reason":"Final supported-path truth sweep complete. SdkLanguage gate added; sweep doc + harness landed in 8697fe0d. Residual: ft-xbnl0.3.5 readback owners still open (tracked, not fake). See bead notes for closure template fields and exact commands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","capability-truth","goal-line","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:37.237406Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:03.850363Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:12.080545Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:37.472465Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:37.697336Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:37.922130Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:38.160071Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:38.402570Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0.4","title":"[EPIC] Mux leak eradication, long-haul reliability proof, and performance closure","description":"# Goal
Get over the original mission line on reliability: eradicate verified mux and runtime leak classes, prove shutdown reconnect restore correctness, and show that large multi-agent workloads remain stable over long durations.

This epic exists because the repo already contains good scaffolding for SLOs and soak confidence, but not yet the level of proof required to say the mux server memory leak problem is solved or this is trustworthy for multi-day swarms.","design":"# Design
1. Instrument first so retention classes become visible instead of anecdotal.
2. Split remediation into mux and server lifecycle leaks versus runtime, scrollback, capture, and search pressure leaks.
3. Back fixes with deterministic leak oracles before relying on long-haul soak.
4. Use soak and benchmark evidence to set real budgets and release gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Known leak-prone lifecycle surfaces are instrumented and measurable.
2. Confirmed mux and runtime leak classes have root-cause fixes and regression coverage.

+{"id":"ft-xbnl0.4","title":"[EPIC] Mux leak eradication, long-haul reliability proof, and performance closure","description":"# Goal
Get over the original mission line on reliability: eradicate verified mux and runtime leak classes, prove shutdown reconnect restore correctness, and show that large multi-agent workloads remain stable over long durations.

This epic exists because the repo already contains good scaffolding for SLOs and soak confidence, but not yet the level of proof required to say the mux server memory leak problem is solved or this is trustworthy for multi-day swarms.","design":"# Design
1. Instrument first so retention classes become visible instead of anecdotal.
2. Split remediation into mux and server lifecycle leaks versus runtime, scrollback, capture, and search pressure leaks.
3. Back fixes with deterministic leak oracles before relying on long-haul soak.
4. Use soak and benchmark evidence to set real budgets and release gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Known leak-prone lifecycle surfaces are instrumented and measurable.
2. Confirmed mux and runtime leak classes have root-cause fixes and regression coverage.
3. Strongly representative soak evidence exists for 50, 100, and 200 pane style workloads.
4. Runtime SLOs and soak confidence operate as hard gates with stored artifacts.
5. Reliability signals produced by this epic are surfaced in a form later operator diagnostics, playbooks, and acceptance scenarios can consume directly.","notes":"The user’s original complaint was not some code still references Tokio. It was that the system should actually work better than legacy WezTerm for large multi-agent workloads. This epic is the proof lane for that claim.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.526396Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T20:17:00.756576Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T20:17:00.756211Z","close_reason":"All child reliability beads ft-xbnl0.4.1 through ft-xbnl0.4.6 are closed with retained evidence: instrumentation inventory, mux/runtime retention fixes, deterministic leak-oracle regressions, soak matrix artifacts, and hard release-gate policy/evaluator surfaces.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","mux","performance","reliability","soak"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.526396Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","title":"Build a leak-risk inventory and instrumentation layer for mux and runtime lifecycles","description":"# Goal
Make leak and retention risk visible before trying to fix it.

This task exists because the project cannot close the original memory-leak complaint by intuition. Pane, tab, window, session, subscription, callback, channel, scope, file-descriptor, and scrollback lifecycles all need instrumentation so future fixes are tied to evidence rather than anecdotes.","design":"# Design
1. Inventory the lifecycle surfaces most likely to retain memory or file descriptors in mux and runtime hot paths.
2. Add counters, gauges, traces, or debug dumps that let those surfaces be observed during churn, reconnect, restore, and long-haul runs.
3. Make the instrumentation cheap enough to use in soak and benchmark harnesses.
4. Feed the resulting metrics into later leak-oracle, soak, and release-gate work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Leak-prone lifecycle surfaces have explicit instrumentation or debug evidence hooks.
2. The instrumentation is documented well enough that future agents can tell what each signal means.
3. Later remediation and soak tasks can rely on this instrumentation instead of inventing their own ad hoc probes.
4. Completion evidence includes at least one representative capture showing the instrumentation in use.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is the measurement prerequisite for the rest of the reliability lane. Without it, remediation claims will stay fuzzy.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCastle","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.263296Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-04T07:01:39.719408Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T07:01:39.719211Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","leaks","observability","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:38.619426Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:38.844333Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:04.112085Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.263296Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2829,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Agent Mail DB calls are still failing for , , and file reservations, so I am using Beads comments as the durable coordination trail while proceeding.  {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","title":"Root-cause and fix mux-server retention and leak classes across pane tab window and session lifecycles","description":"# Goal
Eliminate the mux-server leak and retention classes that undermine the original project mission.

This task exists because the user’s motivating complaint specifically called out mux-server memory leaks. Those leaks are likely to live in lifecycle ownership, registry maps, sessionhandler state, callback or subscription cleanup, detach or reconnect behavior, and shutdown paths.","design":"# Design
1. Use the instrumentation from the preceding bead to isolate concrete mux-server leak classes.
2. Fix lifecycle ownership and cleanup bugs around pane, tab, window, domain, and session state.
3. Pay special attention to reconnect, detach, callback, and subscription cleanup semantics so old state cannot remain reachable forever.
4. Add focused regression coverage that proves the specific retention class is gone.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Confirmed mux-server leak classes covered by this bead have root-cause fixes.
2. Cleanup semantics for the affected lifecycle paths are explicit and test-covered.
3. Instrumentation shows the relevant counts and memory signals return to expected baselines after churn.
4. Completion evidence names the exact leak classes fixed instead of claiming generic improvement.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is the direct answer to the original mux leak complaint. It should not be allowed to close on broad hand-waving.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.684813Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:17.587843Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:17.586607Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","leaks","mux","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:04.371299Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.293064Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.526782Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.684813Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.073260Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","title":"Root-cause and fix runtime, scrollback, capture, search, and file-descriptor retention outside the mux server","description":"# Goal
Eliminate the major non-mux retention and pressure sources that can still sink long-haul agent workloads.

This task exists because even after mux-server leaks are fixed, the system can still fail its mission through scrollback growth, capture backlogs, search caches, descriptor leaks, or long-lived runtime data structures that never release pressure.","design":"# Design
1. Use the instrumentation layer to isolate pressure and retention sources outside the mux server itself.
2. Prioritize scrollback, capture, search, and descriptor lifecycle behavior because those are directly tied to multi-agent workload scale.
3. Fix ownership and reclamation bugs rather than papering over them with ad hoc trimming.
4. Add targeted regression and benchmark evidence for each important fix.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Confirmed runtime-side retention or descriptor classes covered by this bead have root-cause fixes.
2. The fixes are backed by regression coverage and evidence that the relevant counters return to baseline after stress.
3. The resulting behavior is stable enough to support long-haul soak without hidden pressure growth.
4. Completion evidence distinguishes runtime-side fixes from mux-server-side fixes.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"Implemented immediate runtime pane-state compaction in discovery + maintenance and search watermark pruning for disappeared panes.
